---
title: "Early-Season Underdogs"
subtitle: "A public-data audit of Spencer's betting theory"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
---

## Question

- Does betting every early-season underdog at closing moneyline odds make money?
- Sample: NFL, NBA, NHL, MLB from 2011 through 2021.
- Standard: public, reproducible, uncertainty-aware.

## Why This Could Work

- Preseason priors can be stale.
- Sportsbooks and bettors may update slowly after only a few games.
- Small probability mistakes matter more for underdogs because payouts are asymmetric.

## Data and Design

- Public sportsbook archive snapshots from a documented GitHub repository.
- 53,453 cleaned games across 44 league-seasons.
- Main windows:
  - first 2 weeks
  - first 3 weeks
  - first 5 games
  - first 10 percent of the team-season proxy
- Primary stake: $100 flat bet on every qualifying underdog.

## What Would Count as Evidence

- Positive ROI after vig matters, but it is not enough on its own.
- Beating all-season underdogs is suggestive.
- The sharper test is calibration:
  - did underdogs win more often than the no-vig market implied?
- An interval spanning losses and gains means the strategy is not established as a reliable edge.

## Pipeline

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A["Public sportsbook archives"] --> B["Alias cleaning and harmonization"]
    B --> C["Game panel and side-bet expansion"]
    C --> D["ROI, benchmarks, season-proxy checks"]
    C --> E["Calibration, shrinkage, simulation"]
```

## Headline Results

| Rule | ROI | Bets |
|---|---:|---:|
| Underdogs, first 3 weeks | -0.4% | 5,901 |
| All underdogs, likely-regular-season proxy | -1.8% | 49,850 |
| Favorites, first 3 weeks | -4.1% | 5,900 |

95% bootstrap interval for the headline rule: `[-4.0%, 3.4%]`.

Reading rule: the estimate is near breakeven, and the interval still includes meaningful losses and modest gains.

## League Split

| League | ROI | Read |
|---|---:|---|
| MLB | +3.9% | positive hint |
| NFL | +1.8% | noisy hint |
| NBA | -0.4% | near zero |
| NHL | -8.2% | negative |

Partial pooling shrinks the split back toward a slightly negative pooled mean.

## Calibration Evidence

- Early 3-week underdogs:
  - realized win rate: 40.45%
  - no-vig implied win probability: 39.70%
  - calibration gap: +0.75 percentage points
- Interpretation:
  - some mild early underpricing may be present
  - the gap is too small to support a robust cross-league profit claim after vig

## Bottom Line

- Early underdogs slightly outperform their own implied probabilities.
- The effect is too small and unstable to call a durable cross-league edge.
- Best reading:
  - weak support for mild early mispricing
  - strongest in MLB and maybe NFL
  - no credible all-league profit claim
